Ginkgo biloba, commonly known as the maidenhair tree, is a unique and ancient plant species with a long history of use in traditional medicine. Ginkgo biloba is valued for its potential health benefits, including improving cognitive function, memory, and blood flow, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba include flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ginkgo biloba is often used in herbal preparations, such as capsules, tablets, and teas, to support brain health, improve circulation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
